title: Aulomyrcia campestris
taxonomy: Aulomyrcia campestris belongs to the family Myrtaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family comprises over 130 genera and approximately 3,300 species, including several economically important plants. Within the Myrtaceae family, Aulomyrcia campestris is classified under the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Melastomataceae and Penaeaceae. The classification of Aulomyrcia campestris is further supported by its characteristics, such as its ovate leaves and white or yellowish flowers. By classifying Aulomyrcia campestris, botanists can better understand its relationships with other plants and its evolutionary history.

intro: Aulomyrcia campestris, also known as the Amazonian guava tree, is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. The Amazonian guava tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including digestive problems and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
